**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters**

Here's a list of the nomenclature provided, which effectively serves as a glossary of constants and parameters:

*   Bm = mass transfer number
*   BT = heat transfer number
*   C = standard Smagorinsky constant
*   CD = coefficient of drag of a spray particle
*   Ck = turbulent-viscosity constant in the one-equation kinetic-energy model
*   Cε = dissipation term constant in the one-equation kinetic-energy model
*   Сф = model constant in the Interaction by exchange with the mean model
*   срх = gas-phase specific heat coefficient at constant pressure
*   срр = specific heat capacity of a droplet
*   D = molecular diffusivity
*   DT = turbulent diffusivity
*   dk = diameter of the kth spray particle
*   FL = filtered mass density function
*   G = filter kernel
*   Ja = diffusional flux of species α in the i-th coordinate direction
*   k = turbulent kinetic energy
*   ka = gas-phase thermal conductivity
*   kSGS = subgrid scale kinetic energy
*   Ma = subgrid scale mass flux of scalar α
*   mdotk = rate of change of mass of a spray particle
*   p = pressure
*   Rek = Reynolds number of the kth spray particle
*   Sc = turbulent Schmidt number
*   Sij = strain-rate tensor
*   Scont = spray source term in the continuity equation
*   Smomi = spray source term in the momentum equation
*   Ssci = spray source term in the scalar transport equation
*   Soalpha = chemical source term of species α
*   Salpha tilde = filtered reaction rate of scalar α
*   Tij = subgrid stress tensor
*   ui = instantaneous velocity in the i-th coordinate direction
*   u tilde i =  Favre filter of ui
*   Vki = velocity of the kth spray-particle in the i-th coordinate direction
*   xi = Cartesian coordinate in the i direction
*   Δ = filter width
*   δ = Dirac delta function
*   delta ij = Kronecker delta
*   ε = turbulent dissipation
*   μ (or μg)= gas-phase molecular viscosity
*   μt = turbulent viscosity
*   νT = turbulent kinematic viscosity
*   xi = instantaneous mixture fraction
*   ρ (or ρg)= gas-phase density
*   ρp = density of a spray particle
*   ρ tilde = filtered (or mean) density
*   τdj = spray particle relaxation time
*   tauij = filtered viscous stress tensor
*   phi alpha = mass fraction of species α in physical space
*   Phi = filtered value of a scalar phi
*   PSIalpha = mass fraction of species α in composition space
*   omega = mixing frequency

title: 2006_Large-Eddy_Simulations_as_a_Design_Tool_for_Gas_Turbine_Combustion_Systems
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 2]
Here's the breakdown of technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   φ(x, t) = integral [φ'(x', t) * G((x - x') / Δ)] dx'
*   integral[G(ψ) dψ] = 1
*   G(-ψ) = G(ψ)
*   integral[ψ^m * G(ψ) dψ] exist for m >= 0
*   ∂(ρ)/∂t + ∂(ρu_i)/∂x_i = S_m
*   ∂(ρu_i)/∂t + ∂(ρu_i u_j)/∂x_j = - ∂p /∂x_i + ∂τ_ij /∂x_j + S_mj
*   ∂(ρφ_α)/∂t + ∂(ρu_i φ_α)/∂x_i = ∂(pD ∂Φ_α/∂x_i)/∂x_i - ∂M^α/∂x_i + pS_α + S_mα
*   Kinetic Energy = u_i u_i/2

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Δ : filter width
*   ρ : density
*   τ_ij : viscous stress tensor
*   φ_α: scalar array
*   T_ij = ρu_i u_j - ρ u_i ~u_j: subgrid-scale (SGS) stress
*   M^α = ρu_i φ_α - ρ u_i ~φ_α: SGS scalar mass flux
*   S_α : filtered reaction source terms
*   Sm , Smj, Sma: liquid fuel source terms
*   Δx: Grid spacing
*   m: index for integral of "psi" times filter function

title: 2006_Large-Eddy_Simulations_as_a_Design_Tool_for_Gas_Turbine_Combustion_Systems
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Tij - (delta_ij /3) * Tkk = -2 * nu_t * S_ij = -2 * (C*delta)^2 * |S| * S_ij
*   ksgs = 0.5 * (tilde{uiuk} - tilde{ui}*tilde{uk})
*   d/dt (rho * ksgs) + d/dx_j (rho * uj * ksgs) = Psgs - Dsgs + d/dx_i ( (nu_t / Pr_t) * d/dx_i (ksgs) )
*   Psgs = -T_ij * (d/dx_j (ũ_i))
*   T_ij = -2 * rho * Ck * delta_sgs * (tilde{S_ij} - delta_ij/3 * tilde{S_kk}) + 2/3 * rho * ksgs * delta_ij
*   nu_t = C_nu * delta_sgs * k_sgs^(1/2)
*   Dsgs = C_epsilon * p * k^(3/2)/ delta

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 1:** Comparison of mean *u* and *v* velocities with experimental data for lid-driven cavity flow using various models.
    *   **Axes:**
        *   x-axis: x/B (dimensionless)
        *   y-axis: v/Ub (dimensionless)    u/Ub (dimensionless)
   * **Curves:**
        *   localized dynamic (solid line)
        *   kinetic-energy equation (dashed line)
        *   standard Smagorinsky (dash-dotted line)
        *   RANS (short dashed line)
        *   Experimental Data for u velocity (circles)
        *   Experimental Data for v velocity (squares)
    *   **Key Values:** Plots show u/UB and v/UB versus x/B.  Ranges approximately -0.5 to 0.5 for both axes/variables.

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   *C* (Smagorinsky constant) values ranging from 0.065 to 0.17
*   Constant *C* (standard Smagorinsky model, value set to) : 0.17
*   Constant C_nu (kinetic energy model): 0.916
*   Re (Reynolds number): < 5 x 10^3 (laminar),  > 6 x 10^3 (unstable), > 1 x 10^4 (turbulent). Used for simulations: 1 x 10^4
*   SAR (Spanwise Aspect Ratio, L/B): Used for Simulations : 0.5
*   Mesh Resolution: 64 x 64 x 32
*   k_sgs: SGS kinetic energy
*   T_ij: SGS Stress
* delta: filter width
*   S_ij: strain rate tensor
*  nu_t: turbulent viscosity
*  Pr_t: Turbulent Prandtl Number (*not explicitly defined with a specific value, but mentioned in the context of the transport equation.*)
* p: Density
* Ck and C are parameters assumed to be constant or dynamically computed.
* epsilon: turbulent dissipation rate.
* U_B: lid velocity
* tilde{u

title: 2006_Large-Eddy_Simulations_as_a_Design_Tool_for_Gas_Turbine_Combustion_Systems
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text and images:

**1. Mathematical Equations**

*   Re = 2.6 x 10^3
*   4πh × 2h × 2πh

**2. Graphs/Charts**

*   **Fig. 2**: Lid-driven cavity flow turbulence intensities
    *   Axes:
        *   x/B: -0.5 to 0.5
        *   y/B: -0.5 to 0.5
        *   vrms (x 10)/UB:  -0.5 to 0.5
        *   urms (x 10)/UB:  -0.5 to 0.5
    *   Curves: localized dynamic; kinetic-energy equation; standard Smagorinsky; experimental data for u velocity;  experimental data for v velocity
*   **Fig. 3**: Plane turbulent Couette flow mean u velocity
    *   Axes:
        *   y/h: -0.5 to 0.5
        *   u/U_W: 0 to 1
    *   Curves: localized dynamic, kinetic-energy equation, DNS data of Bech et al.; RANS, experimental data of Aydin and Leutheusser
*   **Fig. 4**: Plane turbulent Couette flow rms u velocity
    *   Axes:
        *   |y - h|/h: 0 to 1
        *   urms/Utau: 0 to 3
    *   Curves: localized dynamic model; kinetic-energy model; DNS data of Bech et al.; experimental data of Bech et al.; experimental data of Aydin and Leutheusser

**3. Tables**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams**
*   **Fig 5**: Schematic of Sommerfeld and Qiu experimental configuration
    *   Components: Annular injection tube, nozzle, atomizer, air flow, cylindrical centerbody
**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters**

*   Transitional Reynolds number for plane Couette flow: approximately 300
*   Fully developed turbulence: Reynolds number above 500
*   Heated air inner diameter tube: 200 mm
*   Heated air length tube: 1500 mm
*   Inner and outer diameters of annulus: 40 and 64 mm
*   Air mass flow rate: 29 g/s
*   Air temperature: 373 K
*   Computational domain size: 4πh × 2h × 2πh
*   Grid resolution: 32 x 32 x 32
*   Total Grid Points: 1.2 million

text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 6]
Here is the technical content extracted from the provided text:

1.  **Mathematical Equations:**
    *   dV_k^i / dt = (u_i - V_k^i) / tau_d                  (11)
    *   tau_d = 4d_p rho_p / 3C_D rho_g |u - V^k|  (12)
    *   Re^k = rho_p |u - V^k| d / mu_g                 (13)
    *   m_p = 2 pi d^2 (k / c_p)_g ln(1 + B_m)                   (14)
    *   dT_s / dt = m_p L / (c_pp m) (B_T / B_m - 1)      (15)

2.  **Graphs/Charts:**
    *   **Figure 9**
        *   Axes:
            *   x (m)
            *   u (m/s)
        *   Units: meters (m), meters per second (m/s)
        *   Curves: Spray particles and velocity contours
        *   Key values:
            *   x ranges from -0.1 to 0.5
            *   u ranges from -10 to 15

3.  **Tables:**
    NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

4.  **Diagrams:**
   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

5.  **Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**
    *   Cone angle of the spray: 60 deg
    *   Mean axial velocity: 18 m/s
    *   Sauter mean diameter: 40
    *   Fuel flow rate: 0.44 g/s
    *   Airflow rate: 28.3 g/s
    *   Droplet injection radius: 3 mm

text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 7]
Here's the extracted technical content from the provided text and figures.

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   FL(ψ, x; t) = integral from -infinity to +infinity of rho(x', t)delta[ψ - phi(x, t)]G(x' - x) dx' (16)
*   a/at (FL) + a/axi (u_i FL) = a/apsi_alpha ( (1/rho) a/axi_j (rho xi^a psi) FL )
*   - a/axi [((u_i) psi) - (u_i) (psi)) FL] - (Sa FL) (17)
*   a/apsi_alpha ( (1/rho) a/axi_j (rho xi^a psi) FL ) = [C_omega (psi - (psi)) FL] (18)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Fig 10 and 11: Particle Velocity/Diameter predictions:**
    *   **Axes (Fig 10):**
        *   x-axis: u (m/s)
        *   y-axis: y (m)
    *   **Axes (Fig 11):**
        *   x-axis: d10
        *   y-axis: y (m)
    *   **Curves:** There are three subplots a), b), and c) in each of fig  10 and 11.
        *   Experimental data (marked by dots)
        *   LES predictions (solid lines)
        *   x=0.05m, x=0.1m, x=0.3m are the values for a, b and c

**3. Tables:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   x = 0.05 m, x = 0.1 m, x = 0.3 m (axial locations)
*   C_omega = 2.0 (model constant)
*   psi = scalar variable in composition space (mixture fraction)
*  delta function presented in equations
*  phi = scalar variable in composition space
*  n = reaction progress variable
*  rho = density

I have tried to be as complete and concise as possible. Let me know if you need further refinement.

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   ∂ui/∂t + ∂ui * uj/∂xj = -∂p/∂xi + ∂τij/∂xj - ∂Tij/∂xj
*   Tij = ui * uj - ui * uj

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   Fig. 21a: Production combustor: a) instantaneous velocity magnitude, horizontal axis not indicated; vertical axis: velslv, units: not indicated; curve data: colors vary from black at 0.0 to white at 0.9
*   Fig. 21b: Production combustor: b) temperature fields, horizontal axis not indicated; vertical axis: T/T∞ , units: not indicated; curve data: colors vary from black at 0.0 to white at 0.9

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   Fig. 20: Model of production combustor for LES; 1-million-element grid.
    *   Components: Shows the 3D grid of a gas turbine combustor. No specific labels beyond the title.

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   LES turnaround time: about four days on an eight-processor LINUX machine
*   Grids involving several million cells will have to be used to improve accuracy.
